senses.gloss: to lift, to raise aloft, to hoist up, to heave up

examples.english: She lifted her hands toward the sky.

senses.gloss: to elevate, to heighten, to exalt, to enhance
to fly (a kite), to run up (a flag)
to take off, to doff, to tip (one’s hat)
to remove, to take away, to end, to lift
to become high, to become elevated, to become exalted (of a person; especially in value, position, esteem, etc.)
to become thin, to become fine (of a garment or piece of cloth)
to become loud in voice (of a person)
to lift, to raise, to elevate
pronouncing a final consonant with u as a mark of the nominative case (see desinential inflection) or indicative mood
